[FL1][M] Proposal 5.2-3

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation, adopt the table above as the evaluation assumption for baseline case/combination of sensing vehicle.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
Tiami | Yes | 
IDCC | Yes | 
vivo | Yes | 
Xiaomi | [Yes] | For Urban grid scenario: BS transmit power (per 20 MHz): The BS transmit power is suitable for non TRP-mono cases, the parameter name can be updated with “BS transmit power (per 20 MHz) for non TRP mono-static”. Besides, 49 dBm per 20MHz is preferred to achieve maximum power of 56dBm for 4GHz with 100MHz. As for the note, the descriptions for indoor can be removed since UMa is outdoor only. BS antenna configuration: Since the table is assumptions for baseline, so many options (2 or 3 for one candidate frequency) are not suitable for results comparison. One candidate value can used as baseline, others are optional. “Outdoor Combination 1” for 4GHz, “Outdoor Combination 1” for around 7Ghz, and “Outdoor Combination 1” for 30GHz can be used as baseline. As for the note, it could be revised after discussion of Proposal 5.3-1 UE antenna height: It should be clarified the definition of Fixed UE at Roadside, not sure whether it is the RSU-type UE or CPE. UE antenna configuration: For Handheld UE, config 1 could be selected as baseline for 30GHz For fixed UE at Roadside, CPE and RSU-type UE could be discussed separately. Configuration for CPE could reuse the discussion for UAV and human. For RSU-type UE, for around 7GHz and below, 8T8R as baseline, e.g. (M, N, P, Mg, Ng; Mp, Np) = (1, 4, 2, 1, 1; 1, 4), (dH,dV) = (0.5, 0.8) λ; For 30GHz, 32T32R as baseline, e.g. (M, N, P, Mg, Ng; Mp, Np) = (1, 4, 2, 1, 4; 1, 1), (dH,dV) = (0.5, 0.5) λ. The Tx antenna configuration of UE should also be defined. UE antenna radiation pattern: For Handheld UE, Table 7.3-2 in TR 38.901 is assumed for antenna config with Alt 2 and isotropic radiation power pattern is assumed for Alt1, as agreed in communication evaluation assumption agenda. For CPE UE, three candidate radiation pattern is agreed in communication evaluation assumption agenda, maybe one can be selected as baseline. For vehicle, two radiation patterns are modeled in TR 37.885, option 1 could be used as baseline. UE array orientation: For handheld UE, random in horizontal domain could be used. For vehicle, follow the model for vehicle in TR 37.885 UE distribution: One candidate option could be selected as baseline. For example, option 2 for handheld UE, and option 2 for vehicle UE. For the UE type RSU (fixed UE at road side), option 2 could be used as baseline. UE noise figure: One candidate value of noise figure could be selected as baseline. For example, 9dB for around 7GHz and below and 10dB for around 15GHz and above, which is usually used in NR communication evaluation. 3D distribution: Dropping option A for Urban Grid in 37.885 could be used. Minimum target-target 3D distance: Min vehicle UE to vehicle UE distance in TR 37.775 could be uses, i.e., minimum 2m between the rear bumper of a vehicle and the front bumper of the following vehicle in the same lane.
NEC | Yes | 
DCM | Yes | Regarding RCS model, Option 1: single scattering point (baseline) Option 2: multiple scattering point (optional)
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | A few comments for further consideration/discussion: We are also considering to have ISD 250m for ~7GHz We think the number of targets is too small. It’s better to consider the number of vehicles to approach the realistic deployment. 400MHz can be considered.

For UE antenna configuration, we suggest using CPE antenna configuration for both RSU and vehicle:

Simulation assumption | Selection of sensing Tx-Rx pair | Companies report the option and selection method for STx-SRx pair: Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.). | Companies report the option and selection method for STx-SRx pair: Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.). | Companies report the option and selection method for STx-SRx pair: Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.).

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
Lenovo |  | From sensing perspective, vehicle UEs can not be dropped as “floating antennas” in the space, as they are attached to a physical vehicle target object. The correct dropping improves sensing performance, without using the same vehicle UE to sense the co-located vehicle. We have provided related evaluation in [R1-2604233] where the sensing performance is improved by using the knowledge of sensing UE and target co-location. To cover this, we suggest using the following wording as previously suggested by the FL: Part of the sensing targets (vehicle) may be optionally collocated with some vehicle UEs. When collocated, the UE is not selected as the STx/SRx for this collocated sensing target.
Tiami |  | We agree with Lenovo comment.

The Tx antenna configuration of UE should also be defined. UE antenna radiation pattern: For Handheld UE, Table 7.3-2 in TR 38.901 is assumed for antenna config with Alt 2 and isotropic radiation power pattern is assumed for Alt1, as agreed in communication evaluation assumption agenda. For CPE UE, three candidate radiation pattern is agreed in communication evaluation assumption agenda, maybe one can be selected as baseline. UE array orientation: For handheld UE, random in horizontal domain could be used. UE distribution: One candidate value of N could be selected as baseline. For example, N = 30 for handheld UE, and N = 30 for CPE UE. UE noise figure: One candidate value of noise figure could be selected as baseline. For example, 9dB for around 7GHz and below and 10dB for around 15GHz and above, which is usually used in NR communication evaluation. Minimum target-target 3D distance: Option B can be used as baseline. Additional parameters: The minimum BS-UE distance and minimum UE-UE distance could also be defined. The minimum 2D distances between TRP and the UE is 0 m. The minimum 2D distances between UE and UE is 1 m.

Sensing mode specific assumption

The following table is agreed as the starting point of sensing mode specific assumption in RAN1#124bis [12]:

[TABLE]
Sensing mode | Parameter | Value
TRP mono-static | Sensing Tx/Rx operating simultaneously or not | Simultaneously, or not simultaneously (FFS: with/without switching time and value if any)
TRP mono-static | BS antenna isolation | For Around 7 GHz and below 7 GHz: Option 1: 80 dB Option 2: 65 dB For 30 GHz: Option 1: 100 dB Option 2: 72 dB Option 3: 85 dB
TRP mono-static | BS transmit power | Simultaneously: min { BS transmit power in Table of ISAC system configuration, BS Rx saturation power + BS antenna isolation}, assuming the BS Rx saturation power = -28 dBm Not simultaneously: Follow BS transmit power in the table of general evaluation assumption
TRP mono-static | BS self-interference impact | The residual leakage interference/noise is modelled e.g., by additional additive white Gaussian noise, -174 + 10log10(BW) + X dBm in BW Hz, BW is simulated bandwidth, X = {-Infinity, 0, 5} is up to company report. Note: X = -Infinity corresponds to not modelling self-interference.
UE mono-static | UE type for sensing | Companies to report
UE mono-static | Sensing Tx/Rx operating simultaneously or not | FFS
UE mono-static | UE transmit power | FFS
UE mono-static | UE Antenna isolation | FFS
UE mono-static | UE Self-interference impact | FFS
UE mono-static | Availability of UE location, velocity and orientation | Option 1: Known, ideally (baseline) Option 2: Known, with error. FFS distribution of error in each aspect (e.g. location, speed, orientation). Option 3: Unknown, rely on actual measurement Note: UE location, velocity and orientation are individually determined

TRP-TRP bi-static | Coherency and phase continuity among symbols within a CPI | Option 1: Always coherent Option 2: Coherency is assumed under some conditions; FFS: Conditions
UE-UE bi-static | UE type for sensing | Companies to report

Companies’ input on sensing mode specific discussion is collected as follows:

Simultaneous v.s. non-simultaneous transmission of TRP mono-static

Only half of the AE and TxRU are used when operating simultaneously: ZTE, Qualcomm

Note: no switching time for this case.

Full AE and TxRU are used when operating non-simultaneously, with switching time: Qualcomm, Lenovo

Switching time:

Case 0: 0 us: Lenovo

Case 1: 0.5 us: Qualcomm

Case 2: 3 us: Qualcomm

Case 3: 7 us: Lenovo

Case 4: 10 us: Lenovo

Only simultaneous: ZTE, Spreadtrum, Tiami

FL: Based on companies input, the following proposal is made. Note that, referring to RAN4 requirement is fine. Moreover, RAN4 only set ‘minimum requirement’. The actual capability is usually better than RAN4 requirement. By the way, 0 us seems too optimistic. As middle ground, we may consider 1 us and 5 us, representing optimistic value and conservative value respectively.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.3-1

Revise the content of ‘sensing Tx/Rx operating simultaneously or not’ of TRP mono-static sensing mode as follows.

[TABLE]
Sensing mode | Parameter | Value
TRP mono-static | Sensing Tx/Rx operating simultaneously or not | Simultaneously, or not simultaneously (FFS: with/without switching time and value if any) Option 1: Simultaneously Only half of the antenna elements and TxRUs are used when operating simultaneously No switching time is needed Option 2: Not simultaneously Full antenna elements and TxRUs are used when operating non-simultaneously Switching time Case 1: 1 us Case 2: 5 us

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
Tiami | Yes | Option 1.
IDCC | Yes | Suggest removing the wording “Option” to avoid confusion, i.e., Option 1: Simultaneously Only half of the antenna elements and TxRUs are used when operating simultaneously No switching time is needed Option 2: Not simultaneously Full antenna elements and TxRUs are used when operating non-simultaneously Switching time Case 1: 1 us Case 2: 5 us
vivo | Yes | 
ZTE | Yes with minor comments | Some more antenna configurations should also be considered and different TX&RX antenna configurations would derive different antenna isolation capability and related transmitting power. Option 1: Simultaneously Only half of the antenna elements and TxRUs are used when operating simultaneously No switching time is needed Option 2: Not simultaneously Full antenna elements and TxRUs are used when operating non-simultaneously Switching time Case 1: 1 us Case 2: 5 us
Qualcomm | Yes but | We prefer case 1 to be 0.5 usec.
Xiaomi | [Yes] | Generally fine with the direction of the proposal. We are not sure whether the total number antenna elements of Option 1 should be the same as in Option 2 or if it should be double Option 2. In the SBFD simulation, the double number of antenna elements is used for SBFD when compared with TDD, which could also be used here. With this assumption in mind, total number antenna elements of Option 1 are double of Option 2.
DCM | Yes | Option 1
CMCC | Comment | We are OK to have both options. But we support case-1 being 0.5 us for Option 2, which is align with our deployments. A pulse of 2us with a 0.5us switching time leads to a round-trip distance of 750m approximately, which is basically aligned with the coverage of the common CP@30kHz. Therefore, we suggest to adopt 0.5 us for case-1.
Sony | Yes | 
LGE | Yes | 
Nokia |  | We think option 1 should be considered. As all the antenna will be used for both communication and sensing, we do not think the switching time between Tx and Rx can be that low considering e.g. e.g. a 50m distance between aNB and UE will need a switching time less than 100/3e8=3.33e-7s. Not support Option 2, which needs further feasibility study.
Panasonic | Yes | As a first step, we prefer to start the evaluation with Option 1.
CATT/CICTCI | Yes | 
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Regarding Tx-Rx switching time, 0.5us is doubtful for engineering feasibility. The experimental value is usually 2us.

FL: Based on the offline discussion, the following version is made.

Qualcomm propose the following error modelling for time error:

[TABLE]
UE-UE bistatic: Timing Error between two UEs connected to the same TRP Truncated Gaussian distribution with σ = 10 ns TRP-TRP, TRP-UE, UE-TRP bistatic: Timing Error between two TRPs: Truncated Gaussian distribution with σ = 1 ns (tightly synchronized) σ = 5 ns (conservative scenario)

[TABLE]
Parameter | Proposed values | Distribution | Notes
Timing error (rms) | T1 = {10, 50} ns | Truncated Gaussian, T2 = 2·T1 | Consistent with TR 38.855

ETRI proposes the following error modelling for time error:

[TABLE]
TRP-TRP With timing error, modelled by a truncated Gaussian distribution within ±10 ns for ≤ 7 GHz and ±3 ns for 30 GHz UE-UE Up to companies report TRP-UE Option 2: With error, truncated Gaussian model with σ = 30 ns for timing error UE-TRP Option 2: FFS

Frequency error/offset: vivo (optional), Qualcomm, DOCOMO, ETRI, Tiami

Qualcomm proposes the following error modelling for frequency error:

[TABLE]
TRP-UE, UE-TRP bistatic: Frequency Error at a UE relative to TRP: Before Initial acquisition: Uniform distribution ±5 / ±10 / ±20 ppm After initial acquisition, before fine T/F tracking: Uniform distribution ±0.1 ppm After fine T/F tracking: Uniform distribution ±0.01 ppm Drift rate at a UE: 0.2 ppm/sec Drift rate at a TRP: TBD ppm/sec Frequency Error at a TRP : Uniform distribution ±0.05 ppm

Apple proposes the following error modelling for frequency error:

[TABLE]
Parameter | Proposed values | Distribution | Notes
Frequency error (CFO) | {10, 50} ppb | Per oscillator pair | 10 ppb = high-quality TCXO; 50 ppb = typical UE

ETRI proposes the following error modelling for frequency error:

[TABLE]
TRP-TRP With frequency error (e.g., carrier frequency offset), modelled by uniform distribution +/-0.05 ppm (TR 38.857) UE-UE With frequency error (e.g., carrier frequency offset), modelled by uniform distributions +/-0.1 ppm(UE) TRP-UE Option 2: With error, σ = 0.05 ppm for frequency error Option 3: With frequency error (e.g., carrier frequency offset), modelled by uniform distributions +/-0.05 ppm(TRP) and +/-0.1 ppm(UE) UE-TRP Option 3: With frequency error (e.g., carrier frequency offset), modelled by uniform distributions +/-0.05 ppm(TRP) and +/-0.1 ppm(UE)

Tiami proposes 0.1 ppm for TRP-TRP bi-static and TRP-UE bi-static

FL: Based on companies input, the following proposal is made. Note that, ideal case is strongly preferred by majority, which is reasonable to have a practical, simple start. Moreover, it is indeed several methods to calibrate the error by implementation. It is preferred not to make non-ideal case too complicated at the beginning.

Coherency and phase continuity among symbols within a CPI

Always coherent only/as baseline: vivo, ZTE, CATT/CICTCI, DOCOMO, LGE, Spreadtrum, Sharp, China Telecom

Conditionally breaking coherency: vivo (optional), Qualcomm, Apple, Interdigital, ETRI, Tiami

DL/UL switching: vivo (optional), ETRI, Tiami

Timing drift and TA: vivo (optional)

N (FFS value of N) Breaking events randomly during CPI: Qualcomm (due to multiple reasons)

Qualcomm identifies that coherency can be maintained only if the following conditions are met:

[TABLE]
Category | Condition
RF chains | TX/RX chains remain continuously powered and synchronized
Antennas | No antenna switching within the CPI
Power & calibration | No power adaption, gain change, or RF recalibration during the CPI
Scheduling | No DL/UL switching or TDD direction change inside the CPI
Symbol type | Same symbol type (e.g., DL, UL, SBFD/non-SBFD) throughout the CPI
Resource allocation | Same frequency-domain allocation (RBs/BWP) across the CPI
Other factors | No BWP change, no RRC state change

Limited by coherent time: Apple, ETRI

Apple: T_coh = {1, 5, 10} ms for UE-involved bistatic (TCXO); T_coh = {10, 20, 50} ms for TRP-TRP bistatic (OCXO). Beyond T_coh, non-coherent integration is assumed.

FL: Based on the input, the following proposal is made. ‘Always coherent’ is strongly preferred by majority. At the same time, many conditions and modelling methods are proposed. Among all, DL/UL switching is well supported. But other conditions are also reasonable based on Rel-17 Coverage Enhancement Study, on joint channel estimation. It is preferred to take some typical or simple conditions at the beginning, not to make non-ideal case too complicated.

Availability of UE location, velocity and orientation

Qualcomm proposes the following table, to model the error as truncated Gaussian models for all errors with standard deviation σ and truncated between [-2σ, 2σ]:

[TABLE]
UE type | Location error (2D) | Z-axis Error | Velocity error | Orientation error (yaw, roll, pitch)
CPE | σ = [0.05-0.1] m | σ = [0.05-0.1] m | Perfectly known (static) | σ = [0.1°]
Handheld UE, outdoor | σ = [0.5 – 2] m | σ = [3–6] m | σ = [0.05–0.2] m/s | σ = [2–5°]
Handheld UE, indoor | σ = [0.5 – 3] m | σ = [1–2] m | σ = [0.1–0.5] m/s | σ = [2–5°]
Vehicular UE | σ = [0.1–0.5] m | σ = [0.1–0.5] m | σ = [0.02–0.1] m/s | σ = [0.1°]

Apple proposes the following values:

location error σ = {1, 3, 5} m per axis

velocity error σ = {1, 3} m/s per axis

orientation error σ = {5°, 15°} per axis

Unknown, rely on actual measurement: DOCOMO (remove), ETRI (remove)

FL: Based on the input, the following proposal is made. ‘Known ideally’ is strongly preferred by majority. At the same time, for ‘Know with error’, error ranges are proposed by multiple companies. It is preferred to take some typical or simple conditions at the beginning, not to make non-ideal case too complicated.

Metric and performance target

Metric

The following sensing-related requirements are agreed in RAN plenary and captured in TR 38.914. Note that such 3GPP RAN internal requirements are completely aligned with ITU-R requirements.

[TABLE]
5.1.17 Sensing-related requirements Sensing-related capabilities are measured in terms of the following technical performance requirements: • Detection Probability and False Alarm Probability: Detection probability is the probability of correctly detecting the presence of the sensing target when it is present, and false alarm probability is the associated probability that a sensing target is detected when no corresponding true sensing target is present. • Horizontal/Vertical Localization Accuracy: It is defined as the absolute difference between the estimated horizontal/vertical location and the actual horizontal/vertical location of the sensing target. The value of localization accuracy shall be obtained assuming 90% confidence level, which is the 90th percentile point of the CDF of the location estimation error. Both the horizontal and vertical localization accuracy are estimated based on Cartesian coordinates. • Velocity Accuracy: It is defined as the absolute difference between the estimated velocity and the actual velocity of the sensing target. The value of velocity accuracy shall be obtained assuming 90% confidence level, which is the 90th percentile point of the CDF of the velocity estimation error. In the above technical performance requirements, the sensing target is a passive object, which is not connected to the radio network for communication.

In 5G NR, RAN1 concluded the following performance metrics for sensing, which are nicely captured in TR 38.765 [5]:

[TABLE]
4.1 Definitions of performance metrics In the evaluation of NR ISAC, the following performance metrics are considered: - Horizontal/vertical positioning accuracy is defined as the absolute value of the difference between the estimated horizontal/vertical position and the corresponding true position of a sensing target. NOTE: There should be only one estimated horizontal/vertical position corresponding to the true position of a sensing target. - Velocity accuracy is defined as the absolute value of the difference between the estimated velocity and the corresponding true velocity of a sensing target. For single TRP monostatic sensing, both the radial velocity accuracy and the 3D velocity accuracy can be estimated. The true radial velocity is the projection of true 3D velocity on the direction from TRP to target for TRP monostatic. NOTE: There should be only one estimated velocity corresponding to the true velocity of a sensing target. - Missed detection probability is defined as the conditional probability of not detecting the presence of a target when the target is actually present in the simulation area. where, - is the number of missed targets in the drop n, i.e., the true target not associated with any detected object. - is the number of true targets in the drop n. - is total number of drops with at least one target per drop - False alarm probability Type 1 is defined for cases without true target dropped in simulation area. An object detected when there is no target present in the simulation area is considered a false alarm. where, - is equal to 1 if at least one object is detected when there is no target dropped in the simulation area in the drop n, otherwise is equal to 0. - is the total number of drops without target in the simulation area. - False alarm probability Type 2 is defined for cases with true target dropped in simulation area. An object detected but not associated with any true target in the simulation area is considered as a false alarm. where, - is the number of detected objects but not associated with any true targets in the drop n. - is the total number of detected objects in the drop n. - is number of drops with at least one detected object. - is the total number of drops with target(s) in the simulation area. NOTE: Both False alarm probability types are mandatory.

All the metrics are about object detection and/or tracking. It can be observed that sensing metrics in 5G are aligned with those in ITU-R and RAN Plenary, with some refinement on two types of false alarm probability. Therefore, in FL’s understanding, these metrics are suitable for the evaluation in 6G, in the use cases of object detection and/or tracking. No need to argue whether these metrics are applicable or not.

DOCOMO raised an issue that the False alarm probability Type 2 may be problematic (‘However, the count could include detected objects with high accuracy but not associated with the corresponding true target as the true target is associated with another detected object with higher accuracy. We believe that this count is invalid as in practical, these two detected objects will be identified.’).

[TABLE]
FL | @DOCOMO, this may not be an issue, since in 5G-A we already clarify the following [5], which allows one on one mapping at most. We can open some discussion if such issue is still controversial to you.

Inclusive metric definition for object detection/tracking

Some companies (e.g. CATT/CICTCI, Huawei, Samsung, OPPO, Ericsson, Tiami, etc.) point out that revision is needed to make the metrics more inclusive. The proposals include:

For single pair of bi-static sensing, provide the explanation of ‘radial velocity’;

CATT/CICTCI, OPPO, Ericsson provide very detailed analysis and derivation, and derive that the measurable Doppler at SRx will be: , and subsequently, assuming STx and SRx are static, the formula can be deduced as .

In addition, considering the situation that STx and SRx may be a moving device, the vector projected on incident/scattering direction, is not just the ‘3D velocity vector of the target’, but should be ‘velocity difference vector between 3D velocity vector of the target and the 3D velocity vector of STx/SRx:

,

Replace ‘single TRP mono-static’ with more general description, such as ‘single node mono-static’;

Note that, if we cannot reach clear and inclusive definition of radial velocity for bi-static sensing, companies have to evaluate 3D velocity accuracy for bi-static sensing, as also proposed by Interdigital.

The following proposals are made based on the input.

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.1-1

For object detection and/or tracking, update the definition of velocity accuracy for 6G ISAC evaluation:

Velocity accuracy is defined as the absolute value of the difference between the estimated velocity and the corresponding true velocity of a sensing target. For single node TRP monostatic sensing or single pair of bistatic sensing, both the radial velocity accuracy and the 3D velocity accuracy can be estimated. The true radial velocity is the projection of true 3D velocity on the direction from TRP to target. the sum of the following two components:

Component 1: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the direction from target to STX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the STX;

Component 2: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the direction from target to SRX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the SRX.

Note: If STx and SRx are static, true radial velocity is , where is the true 3D velocity of the target, is the bistatic angle between incident direction and scattering direction, and is the angle between the true 3D velocity vector and the bisector vector within the bistatic angle . For mono-static sensing, .

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments

Statistics for multi-scattering point targets

In Rel-20 NR ISAC study, the sensing target is restricted to UAV, which is modelled only as single scattering point objects. In 6G, there may be an issue that how to make statistics when the target/object is modelled with multi-scattering points, e.g. vehicle, AGV.

In general, there are two possible ways, one is to calculate metrics per target/object, and the other one is to calculate the metrics per scattering point. Quite some companies (e.g. CATT/CICTCI, xiaomi, Interdigital, China Telecom,) think ‘per target/object’ should be adopted. What’s more, some companies also propose to use single scattering point in Rel-20 evaluation, which can avoid this issue.

[TABLE]
FL | FL agrees the analysis from the companies above. Since the performance target from RAN Plenary and ITU-R does not differentiate single scattering point or multi-scattering point, statistics of the metrics should be ‘per target/object’ by natural, unless they clearly stated otherwise. If it is still controversial, we can open the discussion if necessary after other essential issues have sufficient progress.

Existing metrics for tracking

For tracking, while the accuracy of localization and velocity estimation can reuse those for detection (i.e. per point per time stamp), the missed detection or false alarm probability may be defined in trajectory level (CATT/CICTCI) or per target/object level in individual time (xiaomi, Interdigital, China Telecom,).

[TABLE]
FL | Based on the discussion in RAN1#124bis [6], some companies mentioned that current statistics is also applicable to both detection and tracking (per point per CPI/time stamp). Reusing the same statistic granularity can also provide visible difference between object detection and tracking. Unless serious issue is identified, FL think we can assume legacy statistics method (per point per CPI/time instance) still applies to tracking.

Other metrics for object detection and/or tracking

Other than the existing performance metrics, some companies propose to introduce new metrics to reflect the quality of trajectory from tracking. For example:

Sensing latency: Nokia, Qualcomm, T-Mobile USA, Lenovo, Google,

Not needed since latency is E2E requirement: xiaomi,

Sensing refreshing rate: Nokia, Lenovo, T-Mobile USA, InterDigital,

Already reflected by overhead: xiaomi,

Sensing resolution: Nokia, T-Mobile USA, Google

Sensing overhead/CPI: Google

Sensing capacity: Google

Sensing range accuracy: Apple

Classification accuracy and counting resolution: NTHU

Tracking specific metric (e.g., consistency, integrity): CATT/CICTCI

Signal related metric (e.g. PAPR, AF, RF emission, correlation, etc.): Qualcomm

Link level/signal level metrics (CP, power, etc.): NEC

System level communication metrics (throughput, BLER, etc.): Tejas, NEC,

Integration/harmonization between sensing & communication: Qualcomm, NEC

Joint Mutual Information (JMI): PCL

[TABLE]
FL | Recall that, there is no clear performance requirement for any potential new metrics from RAN Plenary or ITU-R so far. Some metrics have been discussed in Rel-20 NR ISAC but not agreeable. Some metrics are from SA1 but usually SA1’s requirement is from view of service rather than RAN1 simulation. Also, some of them seem to be ‘intermediate metrics’, not directly reflecting the performance of object detection and/or tracking, but provide some insight of sensing indirectly. In RAN1#124bis [6] and also this meeting, there is no enough support for any of the metric listed above. Given the situation and limited time, it is suggested focusing on essential issues and existing metrics first. Discussion on additional metrics may be opened only if we have sufficient progress.

Metrics for communication assistance

Performance target

The following sensing-related requirements are agreed in RAN plenary and captured in TR 38.914 [2]. Note that such 3GPP RAN internal requirement is completely aligned with ITU-R requirement.

[TABLE]
5.1.17 Sensing-related requirements Sensing-related capabilities are measured in terms of the following technical performance requirements: • Detection Probability and False Alarm Probability: Detection probability is the probability of correctly detecting the presence of the sensing target when it is present, and false alarm probability is the associated probability that a sensing target is detected when no corresponding true sensing target is present. • Horizontal/Vertical Localization Accuracy: It is defined as the absolute difference between the estimated horizontal/vertical location and the actual horizontal/vertical location of the sensing target. The value of localization accuracy shall be obtained assuming 90% confidence level, which is the 90th percentile point of the CDF of the location estimation error. Both the horizontal and vertical localization accuracy are estimated based on Cartesian coordinates. • Velocity Accuracy: It is defined as the absolute difference between the estimated velocity and the actual velocity of the sensing target. The value of velocity accuracy shall be obtained assuming 90% confidence level, which is the 90th percentile point of the CDF of the velocity estimation error. In the above technical performance requirements, the sensing target is a passive object, which is not connected to the radio network for communication. The minimum requirements for Sensing-related requirements are summarized in Table 5.1.17-1. The minimum requirements for Sensing-related requirements are summarized in Table 5.1.17-1.

The exact Table 5.1.17-1 in TR 38.914 is copied below:

Table 5.1.17-1: Sensing-related requirements

[TABLE]
 | Indoor Factory | Urban Macro
Detection Probability | 95% | 95%
False Alarm Probability | 5% | 5%
Horizontal Localization Accuracy | 2 m | 5 m
Vertical Localization Accuracy | N/A | 8 m (Note 1)
Velocity Accuracy | 2 m/s | 4 m/s

Note 1: The applicability of vertical localization accuracy depends on the type of sensing target.

Performance target for object detection and/or tracking in UMa and Indoor Factory

As highlighted in cyan, the requirements are set assuming 90% confidence level. Hence it is better to clarify this condition in RAN1 to make the target performance clear.

Detection probability = 1 - Missed Detection Probability, naturally. Hence, the required Missed Detection Probability should be 5% (derived from 1 – 95%).

There is another issue regarding false alarm probability. RAN1 defined two types of false alarm probability, i.e. Type 1 and Type 2, corresponding to ‘without target’ condition and ‘with target’ condition, respectively. But RAN Plenary and ITU-R only mention one requirement, i.e. 5%. OPPO proposes to drop Type 1 since the current definition is more closed to Type 2 in their reading. Some other companies (e.g. DOCOMO, Lenovo, Tejas, Qualcomm, InterDigital,) indicate that 5% can be assumed for both Type 1 and Type 2. From FL perspective, to be safe, we can assume that this requirement (5%) can be applied to both False Alarm Probability Type 1 and Type 2.

Note that current requirements do not differentiate sensing target/objects or frequencies. These values have been debated for a long time in ITU-R and RAN Plenary and finally converged. Some companies (e.g. Huawei, Qualcomm, NIST) propose new values for specific sensing targets, which are also discussed in some contributions but no clear suggestion (e.g. Samsung, Nokia). FL suggests no target/object specific requirements at this time, unless clearly required by RAN Plenary or ITU-R.

Speaking of this, RAN Plenary’s requirement clearly states that applicability of vertical localization accuracy depends on the type of sensing target. As mentioned by quite some companies (e.g. InterDigital, Ericsson), it is better to clarify that this is only applicable to the case of sensing UAV.

All in all, the following proposal is made based on the observations.

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.2-1

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation, for object detection and/or tracking, regarding the performance target in UMa and Indoor Factory scenarios given by RAN Plenary:

The performance objective on False Alarm Probability (5%) applies to both False Alarm Probability Type 1 and False Alarm Probability Type 2.

The vertical localization accuracy is only applicable to the case of sensing UAV.

Performance target for object detection and/or tracking in other scenarios

RAN Plenary and ITU-R only set requirements for the scenarios of Indoor Factory and UMa.

Some companies (e.g. Apple) indicate due to some limitation like form factor of UE or ill-condition, the performance target may not be met.

Some companies (DOCOMO, vivo, xiaomi, ZTE, etc.) suggest extending the current requirements of InF and UMa to other indoor and outdoor scenarios, respectively. Ericsson proposes jointly consider RANP/ITU-R requirement and SA TR 22.870.

[TABLE]
FL | No doubt that it is RAN1’s obligation to fulfil the study task from RAN Plenary and ITU-R, while RAN1’s study scope is not limited to these two scenarios. Current requirements on Indoor Factory and UMa are converged after long-time tough discussion in ITU-R. RAN Plenary directly adopted these values in RAN#111. In current requirement for UMa and Indoor Factory, all possible sensing targets, frequencies and conditions have been taken into account. It’s sceptical whether we can tune the requirements a little higher or lower on top of existing values for other scenarios, and what’s underlining message with the modified values. For other scenarios, with or without performance target does not impact RAN1 study on evaluation, including evaluation methodology, assumptions, simulation and performance statistics, as well as channel modelling. Therefore, FL suggests postponing the discussion to (1) see whether RAN Plenary will give additional guidance in Jun. 2026, (2) monitor the overall progress in other essential aspects.

Extension of channel model for sensing

Considering the very limited TU, we need to be extremely careful on the study direction and the scope of channel modeling extension.

Trajectory other than uniform linear motion

Trajectory was discussed a bit in Rel-19, but not widely studied. In Rel-19, for all sensing targets, only constant velocity is agreed. And hence, by default, the trajectories for all sensing targets are uniform linear motion derived from constant velocity, without any disturbance. We also call it ‘straight-line trajectory’ which is captured in TR 38.901 [7].

Some companies think such ‘straight-line trajectory’ is sufficient for Rel-20 study:

Straight-line trajectory: MTK, xiaomi, CATT/CICTCI, Ericsson, OPPO,

Some companies think trajectory other than straight-line trajectory (uniform linear motion derived from constant velocity, without disturbance) is needed for the study, especially for the use case of tracking. At the same time, some other companies have the opposite view. These companies show preference on new trajectory model in Rel-20:

Kinematic/maneuvering models, e.g. state-transition equation with CV + CA + additive process noise, in tangent and/or radial direction: Nokia, vivo, ZTE (noise only)

Note: companies use different but similar terms to describe such non-linear trajectory. We can discuss the suitable terms, if necessary.

In last meeting, majority companies do not think trajectory other than uniform linear motion is needed [6]. The situation is not changed in this meeting. Meanwhile, it should be noted that tracking is in Rel-20 scope. The following proposal is suggested as a compromise.

[FL1][H] Proposal 7.2-1

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation, for tracking sensing targets/objects, consider the following trajectory model:

Baseline: Straight-light trajectory, i.e. uniform linear motion derived from constant velocity without disturbance

Other trajectories can be optionally reported by companies

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
Tiami | Yes | We agree with the baseline.
IDCC | Yes | We support the FL proposal
vivo | No | If straight-light trajectory is the assumption, in theory, we don’t need to have any measurements for tracking. This is because tracking estimator, e.g., Kalman filter, will put 100% trusty on state equation. For example, with Kalman filter, after a while, updated K gain becomes zero due to zero noise assumption on state equation.
OPPO | Yes | 
ZTE | No | Straight-light trajectory would make the evaluation on tracking unconvincing. If it is used, as long as velocity is estimated, position of sensing target on any time point could be derived by starting position and total time step. That’s what will happen in Kalman filter as vivo said. Then the sensing algorithm would generate sensing result based on historical measurements, not based on current measurements, making sensing as “purely predicting”. We propose to use constant velocity(CV) model with velocity noise as baseline, which is already widely used in radar field.
Ericsson | OK | Straight line trajectory is Constant Velocity model if we understood correctly.
Xiaomi | Yes | We are fine with the baseline
NEC | Yes | 
DCM | Yes | 
Apple | Yes | 
Sony | Yes | 
LGE | Yes | We support the baseline. As a minor point, there appears to be a typo where "straight-line trajectory" is written as "straight-light trajectory".
NIST |  | Agree with vivo and ZTE’s concerns
Nokia |  | For a simple straight-line trajectory, we think there can be constant velocity or constant acceleration as the baseline for a real use case. We propose to update the proposal as “For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation, for tracking sensing targets/objects, consider the following trajectory model: Baseline: Straight-line trajectory, i.e. uniform linear motion derived from constant velocity or constant acceleration without disturbance Other trajectories can be optionally reported by companies”
Spreadtrum | Yes | 
Panasonic | Yes | 
CATT/CICTCI | Yes |

I remove the term ‘optionally’. Anyway it is up to you to bring the results. | Given the feedback, the following proposal is made. @vivo, ZTE, NIST, Nokia, I understand your view. However, please look at the reply from majority and consider the vast majority view. I remove the term ‘optionally’. Anyway it is up to you to bring the results.

LLS channel model

Views on LLS channel modeling were collected in RAN1#124bis [6]. On how to model LLS channel, the following methods were discussed by companies in this meeting:

Option 1: Enhanced CDL/TDL model

Option 1-1: Based on existing LLS channel model (CDL or TDL), and add one or more clusters representing target(s)

Option 1-2: Based on existing LLS channel model (CDL or TDL), and update the parameters of one or more cluster to represent target(s)

Option 2: Simplified system level evaluation

Option 2-1: Reducing the system level channel model for ISAC to single target, reduced Tx and Rx

Option 2-2: Limiting the system level simulation to non-cooperative sensing

Option 3: The Tx-target link and target-Rx link are modelled by separate CDL channels, concatenation is then performed to get the target channel; the background channel is modelled by another CDL channel.

In this meeting, companies’ input on LLS channel modeling is summarized as follows:

Support in general: CMCC, Nokia, Lenovo,

Option 1:

Option 1-1: xiaomi, ZTE, InterDigital,

Option 1-2: InterDigital, Tejas,

Option 2:

Option 2-1: ZTE, Apple, MTK, Spreadtrum, ETRI

Option 2-2: xiaomi

Option 3:

It seems Option 2-1 is slightly preferred by majority. Strictly speaking, Option 2-1 is not an LLS channel modelling, but simplified SLS. The largest advantage of Option 2-1 is that no channel model extension is needed – remaining work is to define the simplified evaluation assumptions.

Note that the discussion on LLS channel modeling will be opened only if the necessity is confirmed in Agenda 10.8.2 or Agenda 10.8.3.

[POSTPONE] Proposal 7.3-1

(Place holder for discussing the direction of LLS channel modelling/SLS simplification, depending on the conclusion on necessity of LLS/simplified SLS from AI 10.8.2 or 10.8.3)

Annex: All agreements under this agenda

RAN1 #124bis

Working Assumption

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation,

evaluate object detection and/or tracking for the following sensing targets/objects: UAV, human, vehicle, AGV

discuss further on evaluation on communication assistance

Conclusion

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation, the attributes (e.g. antenna pattern, mobility, etc.) of different UEs are for the purpose of evaluation discussion. No intention to study or specify diverse device type.

Agreement:

For Rel-20 6G sensing performance evaluation of object detection and/or tracking, the following cases/combinations are considered as baseline cases/combinations.

[TABLE]
Sensing target | Indoor or outdoor | Scenario | Carrier frequency
UAV | Outdoor | UMa-AV, SMa | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz (for UMa-AV only)
Human | Outdoor | UMa | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz
Human | Indoor | Indoor Hotspot | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz
Vehicle | Outdoor | Urban Grid, Highway | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz (for Urban Grid only)
AGV | Indoor | Indoor factory (SH) | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz

Note: sensing mode(s) for each row will be discussed

Other cases/combinations can also be evaluated, reported and collected from companies.

Note: In this agreement, no intention to de-prioritize other scenarios or carrier frequencies.

Agreement:

Companies are encouraged to bring input to the evaluation on communication assistance in next meeting, including but not limited to:

Necessity of evaluation on communication assistance

For proponents, provide: what to sense and, if necessary, what/how to assist communication

What metric of sensing is to be defined

Whether/what metric related to communication is to be defined

Whether performance target/KPI is to be defined, for either sensing metric or communication metric

Necessary channel model extension

Necessary update of evaluation methodology on top of 5G NR sensing evaluation methodology

Note: X = -Infinity corresponds to not modelling self-interference.

Note: ‘Availability of UE location, velocity and orientation’ is separately discussed.

Note2: The above values are only for RAN1 sensing evaluation purpose, and the feasibility has not been verified in RAN4.

Agreement:

For object detection and/or tracking, define velocity accuracy for 6G ISAC evaluation:

Velocity accuracy is defined as the absolute value of the difference between the estimated velocity and the corresponding true velocity of a sensing target. For single node monostatic sensing or single pair of bistatic sensing, both the radial velocity accuracy and the 3D velocity accuracy can be estimated. The true radial velocity is the sum of the following two components:

Component 1: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the unit-norm vector from target to STX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the STX;

Component 2: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the unit-norm vector from target to SRX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the SRX.

Note: If STx and SRx are static, true radial velocity is , where is the true 3D velocity of the target, is the bistatic angle between incident direction and scattering direction, and is the angle between the true 3D velocity vector and the bisector vector within the bistatic angle . For mono-static sensing, .

Agreement:

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation, for object detection and/or tracking, regarding the performance target in UMa and Indoor Factory scenarios given by RAN Plenary:

The vertical localization accuracy requirement is applicable to the case of sensing UAV.

Agreement:

## R1-2605102 - R1-2605102_Moderator (CATT)_Moderator summary #6 on ISAC evaluations.docx
Source: E:\标准相关资料\RAN1_125_ISAC_文档整理_20260616\RAN1_125_ISAC\01_Evaluations\Moderator_Summaries\R1-2605102_Moderator (CATT)_Moderator summary #6 on ISAC evaluations.docx

3GPP TSG RAN WG1 #125 R1-2605102

Dalian, China, May 18th – 22nd, 2026

Agenda item: 10.8.1

Source: Moderator (CATT)

Title: Moderator summary #6 on ISAC evaluations

Document for: Discussion/Decision

Plan for next meeting

Thanks everyone for your constructive discussion. Your flexibility and compromise spirit are the key factors to the huge progress in this agenda in this meeting. With that, we can start our sensing evaluation homework.

In next meeting, FL plans to discuss the following issues with high priority.

Necessary update for each general evaluation assumption table, if any.

Necessary update for sensing mode specific assumption table, if any.

Further discussion on SAC (sensing assisted communication).

Channel model extension, probably for AI 10.8.2, if needed.

Necessary clarification on metric, KPI or performance target, if needed.

Have a nice summer break! See you in Maastricht.

Introduction

In 6G Radio study, RAN1 is tasked to evaluate sensing performance and if necessary, extend channel modelling for the selected use cases [1]. The relevant scenarios and requirements can be found in [2]. Per RAN1 Chairman’s guidance, evaluation of sensing in this meeting is planned as follows.

[TABLE]
10.8.1 Evaluations Note 1: Including deployment scenarios, performance evaluation methodologies/metrics/KPIs for the target use cases, and potential extension of channel model, if necessary

The following email thread is assigned for this agenda:

[TABLE]
[125-R20-6GR-ISAC-Evaluations] Email discussion on Rel-20 6GR-ISAC-Evaluations–Yongqiang (CATT) To be used for sharing updates on online/offline schedule, details on what is to be discussed in online/offline sessions, tdoc number of the moderator summary for online session, etc.

This document summarizes the contributions and discussions on Agenda 10.8.1 (Evaluations) in this meeting.

Proposal marks

In this document, issues, questions and proposals are tagged and colored with the following rules.

The proposals in this document are tagged and color coded respectively

[H] Proposal: High Priority, to be handled firstly.

[M] Proposal: Medium Priority, which may be handled on best effort basis or with clear majority view.

[L] Proposal: Low Priority, which may be treated only if there is clear consensus.

In current round for discussion, issues to be discussed are further tagged with [FL5]. Note: In case there is more than one round discussion, some proposals without update are still marked with early round number. If any company hasn’t provided a comment yet, or prefer to update the comments, feel free to comment on them.

File uploading

When uploading draft files for email discussion, if needed, please follow the naming convention in this example:

ISACEvaFLS-v000-Moderator.docx

ISACEvaFLS-v001-Moderator-CompanyA.docx

ISACEvaFLS-v002-CompanyA-CompanyB.docx

ISACEvaFLS-v003-CompanyB-CompanyC.docx

If needed, you may “lock” a spreadsheet file for 30 minutes by creating a checkout file, as in this example:

Assume CompanyC wants to update ISACEvaFLS-v002-CompanyA-CompanyB.docx.

CompanyC uploads an empty file named ISACEvaFLS-v003-CompanyB-CompanyC.checkout

CompanyC checks that no one else has created a checkout file simultaneously, and if there is a collision, CompanyC tries to coordinate with the company who made the other checkout (see, e.g., contact list below).

CompanyC then has 30 minutes to upload ISACEvaFLS-v003-CompanyB-CompanyC.docx

If no update is uploaded in 30 minutes, other companies can ignore the checkout file.

Note that the file timestamps on the server are in UTC time.

Online proposals

Monday

N/A

Tuesday

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.1-1on

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation of object detection and/or tracking, for the baseline cases/combinations, encourage companies to improve the sensing performance, e.g. by exploring different sensing modes.

Note: Evaluating any sensing mode in this agenda does not necessarily mean any RAN1 specification impact.

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.2-1on

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation on communication assistance, at least the following SAC (sensing assisted communication) evaluation scope is considered:

Evaluate the performance of sensing measurement/results, where the sensing measurement/result may be able to assist communication.

Scenario: Urban Grid

Sensing measurement/result to assist communication may include, e.g., delay/Doppler/angle/power related information of the channel, information of reconstructed wall/building, etc. FFS details.

FFS detailed evaluation assumptions, metric, etc.

Up to company to evaluate and report communication performance assisted by sensing.

Company should at least use realistic sensing measurement/result to assist communication.

Company should use the same scenario and ISAC channel as sensing performance evaluation.

Up to company to report communication-related metric and the corresponding performance gain.

[FL1][H] Proposal 7.2-1on

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation, for tracking sensing targets/objects, consider the following trajectory model:

Baseline: Straight-line constant velocity trajectory

Other trajectories can also be reported by companies

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.1-1on

For object detection and/or tracking, update the definition of velocity accuracy for 6G ISAC evaluation:

Velocity accuracy is defined as the absolute value of the difference between the estimated velocity and the corresponding true velocity of a sensing target. For single node TRP monostatic sensing or single pair of bistatic sensing, both the radial velocity accuracy and the 3D velocity accuracy can be estimated. The true radial velocity is the projection of true 3D velocity on the direction from TRP to target. the sum of the following two components:

Component 1: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the unit-norm vector from target to STX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the STX;

Component 2: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the unit-norm vector from target to SRX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the SRX.

Note: If STx and SRx are static, true radial velocity is , where is the true 3D velocity of the target, is the bistatic angle between incident direction and scattering direction, and is the angle between the true 3D velocity vector and the bisector vector within the bistatic angle . For mono-static sensing, .

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.2-1on

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation, for object detection and/or tracking, regarding the performance target in UMa and Indoor Factory scenarios given by RAN Plenary:

The performance objective on False Alarm Probability (5%) applies to both False Alarm Probability Type 1 and False Alarm Probability Type 2.

The vertical localization accuracy requirement is only applicable to the case of sensing UAV.

[ON2][M] Proposal 5.3-1a-on

Revise the content of ‘sensing Tx/Rx operating simultaneously or not’ of TRP mono-static sensing mode as follows.

[TABLE]
Sensing mode | Parameter | Value
TRP mono-static | Sensing Tx/Rx operating simultaneously or not | Simultaneously Part of the antenna elements and TxRUs are used for transmission and the other part is used for reception when operating simultaneously No switching time is needed Not simultaneously Full antenna elements and TxRUs are used for transmission and reception when operating non-simultaneously Switching time Alt 1: 0.5 us Alt 2: 2 us

Note: The above values are only for RAN1 sensing evaluation purpose, and the feasibility has not been verified in RAN4.

[TABLE]

Simulation assumption | Inter-site interference | Option 1: Not modelled Option 2: Explicitly modelled Including interference from communication signal, if applicable. | Option 1: Not modelled Option 2: Explicitly modelled Including interference from communication signal, if applicable.
Simulation assumption | Co-site inter-sector interference | Option 1: Not modelled Option 2: Explicitly modelled Including interference from communication signal, if applicable. | Option 1: Not modelled Option 2: Explicitly modelled Including interference from communication signal, if applicable.
Simulation assumption | Concatenation of TX-target and target-RX links | The NLOS rays in the STX-SPST link are 1-by-1 randomly coupled with the NLOS rays in the ST-SRX link. | The NLOS rays in the STX-SPST link are 1-by-1 randomly coupled with the NLOS rays in the ST-SRX link.
Simulation assumption | The power threshold for path dropping after concatenation for target channel | -40 dB for concatenation | -40 dB for concatenation
Simulation assumption | CPI length | Companies to report | Companies to report
Simulation assumption | Periodicity of CPI for the same STx/SRx pair | Companies to report | Companies to report
Simulation assumption | Number of CPI within one period for the same STx/SRx pair | Companies to report | Companies to report
Simulation assumption | Overhead of sensing resource | Both Overhead 1 and Overhead 2 ((defined in TR 38.765) shall be reported Overhead 1 (Type_1 + Type_3) over all radio DL and UL resources Overhead 2 (Type_1 – Type_2 + Type_3) over all radio DL and UL resources Overhead 2 less than 10% is encouraged | Both Overhead 1 and Overhead 2 ((defined in TR 38.765) shall be reported Overhead 1 (Type_1 + Type_3) over all radio DL and UL resources Overhead 2 (Type_1 – Type_2 + Type_3) over all radio DL and UL resources Overhead 2 less than 10% is encouraged
Simulation assumption | Selection of sensing Tx-Rx pair | Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Note: Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.). | Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Note: Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.).
Simulation assumption | Tx/Rx beam information, (mt x nt) / (mr x nr) | Companies report the Tx and Rx beam information, of BS and/or UE, when applicable. | Companies report the Tx and Rx beam information, of BS and/or UE, when applicable.
Simulation assumption | Algorithm for detection | Companies report the algorithm for detection, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used | Companies report the algorithm for detection, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used
Simulation assumption | Algorithm for tracking | Companies report the algorithm for tracking, if applicable, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used | Companies report the algorithm for tracking, if applicable, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used
Simulation assumption | Other additional features | Optional Up to companies report (e.g. low power clutter, clutter mobility). | Optional Up to companies report (e.g. low power clutter, clutter mobility).

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation, adopt the following evaluation assumption table for baseline case/combination of sensing human.

Simulation assumption | Selection of sensing Tx-Rx pair | Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Note: Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.). | Option 1: a same target is modelled in the channel of single Sensing Tx/Rx Option 2: a same target is modelled in the channel of multiple Sensing Txs/Rxs Option 3: a same target is modelled in the channel of all Sensing Txs/Rxs Note: Selection of STx-SRx pair shall be based on achievable measurement information (e.g. RSRP, pathloss, RSRPP, etc.), but not genie-aided information of the target (e.g. location of the target, range between target and STx/SRx, etc.).
Simulation assumption | Tx/Rx beam information, (mt x nt) / (mr x nr) | Companies report the Tx and Rx beam information, of BS and/or UE, when applicable. | Companies report the Tx and Rx beam information, of BS and/or UE, when applicable.
Simulation assumption | Algorithm for detection | Companies report the algorithm for detection, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used | Companies report the algorithm for detection, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used
Simulation assumption | Algorithm for tracking | Companies report the algorithm for tracking, if applicable, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used | Companies report the algorithm for tracking, if applicable, including whether/how fusion of sensing data from multiple SRx is used
Simulation assumption | Other additional features | Optional Up to companies report (e.g. low power clutter, clutter mobility). | Optional Up to companies report (e.g. low power clutter, clutter mobility).

[ON3][H] Proposal 4.2-1c

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation on communication assistance, at least the following SAC (sensing assisted communication) evaluation scope is considered:

Further discuss the sensing measurement/result used to assist communication, which may include, e.g.:

Delay/Doppler/angle/power related information of the ISAC channel

Information of detected objects (static or moving) and/or large size surface (e.g. EO type-2)

Companies are encouraged to evaluate the performance of sensing measurement/results used to assist communication.

Scenario: Urban Grid with EO type-2.

Urban Grid without EO type-2 and other scenarios are not precluded.

FFS detailed evaluation assumptions, sensing modes, prior information of EO-type2, etc.

Companies to report:

Details of performance metrics on sensing measurement/result used to assist communication

How such sensing measurement/result is able to assist the communication

The corresponding performance

Companies are encouraged to evaluate and report communication performance assisted by sensing.

SAC use cases that agreed in AI 10.8.2 are considered as starting point.

Company should clarify whether ideal/non-ideal sensing measurement/result is used to assist communication.

Company should use the same scenario and ISAC channel as sensing performance evaluation.

Company to report:

Communication-related metric

Detailed methods on how the sensing measurement/result assist the communication

The corresponding performance gain

Friday

FFS: AGV UE as sensing capable UE

[ON4][H] Proposal 4.2-1d

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation on communication assistance, the following SAC (sensing assisted communication) evaluation scope is considered:

Further discuss the sensing measurement/result used to assist communication, which may include, e.g.:

Delay/Doppler/angle/power/phase related information of the ISAC channel

Information of detected objects (static or moving) and/or large size surface (e.g. EO type-2)

Further discuss evaluation of the performance of sensing measurement/results, which are used to assist communication.

Scenario: At least Urban Grid is considered

Urban Grid with or without EO type-2 can both be considered

Other scenarios are not precluded.

FFS detailed evaluation assumptions, sensing modes, prior information of EO-type2, etc.

When evaluated, company should report:

Performance metrics on sensing measurement/result used to assist communication

How such sensing measurement/result is able to assist the communication

The corresponding performance

Further discuss evaluation of communication performance assisted by sensing.

SAC use cases that observed in AI 10.8.2 are considered as starting point, e.g.

Beam management

CSI acquisition

When evaluated, company should use the same scenario and ISAC channel as sensing performance evaluation.

When evaluated, company should clarify whether ideal/non-ideal sensing measurement/result is used to assist communication.

When evaluated, company should report:

Communication-related metric

Detailed methods on how the sensing measurements/results assist the communication

The corresponding performance gain

Note: Strive for minimizing/no extension on existing channel model.

Offline proposals

Monday

There is no offline on Monday.

Tuesday

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.1-1a

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation of object detection and/or tracking, for the baseline cases/combinations, the following sensing modes are considered for the baseline cases/combinations.

[TABLE]
Sensing target | Indoor or outdoor | Scenario | Carrier frequency | Sensing mode
UAV | Outdoor | UMa-AV, SMa | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz (for UMa-AV only) | TRP mono-static, TRP-TRP bi-static, TRP-UE bi-static (UAV UE?)
Human | Outdoor | UMa | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static TRP-TRP bi-static
Human | Indoor | Indoor Hotspot | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static UE mono-static, TRP-TRP bi-static
Vehicle | Outdoor | Urban Grid, Highway | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz (for Urban Grid only) | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static, UE-UE bi-static, TRP mono-static
AGV | Indoor | Indoor factory (SH) | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static TRP mono-static

Note: Other sensing modes (including multi-static sensing) for a baseline case/combination are not precluded.

Note2: Evaluating any sensing mode in this agenda does not necessarily mean any RAN1 specification impact.

Note3: Sensing modes listed in the table may not have the best performance for baseline cases. Encourage companies to improve the performance, e.g. exploring different sensing modes.

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.2-1a

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation on communication assistance, at least the following SAC (sensing assisted communication) evaluation scope is considered:

Evaluate the performance of sensing measurement/results, where the sensing measurement/result may be able to assist communication.

Scenario: Urban Grid with EO type-2

Sensing measurement/result to assist communication, may include, e.g. delay/Doppler/angle/power related information of the channel, information of reconstructed wall/building, etc. FFS details.

FFS detailed evaluation assumptions, metric, etc.

Up to company to evaluate and report communication performance assisted by sensing.

Company should at least use realistic sensing measurement/result to assist communication.

Company should use the same scenario and ISAC channel as sensing performance evaluation.

Up to company to report communication-related metric and the corresponding performance gain.

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.2-2a

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation on communication assistance, further discuss the following potential candidate approaches to assist communication:

Approach 1: Utilize sensing measurement/result to assist the communication performance.

Sensing measurement/result may include, e.g. delay/Doppler/angle/power related information of the channel, information of reconstructed wall/building, etc.

Approach 2: Utilize sensing resource/RS to perform communication-related measurement, and then assist the communication performance.

E.g., Perform CSI/beam-related measurement on sensing resource/RS, to assist CSI acquisition and/or beam management.

[FL1][H] Proposal 7.2-1a

For Rel-20 6G ISAC evaluation, for tracking sensing targets/objects, consider the following trajectory model:

Baseline: Straight-line constant velocity trajectory

Other trajectories can also be reported by companies

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.1-1a

For object detection and/or tracking, update the definition of velocity accuracy for 6G ISAC evaluation:

Velocity accuracy is defined as the absolute value of the difference between the estimated velocity and the corresponding true velocity of a sensing target. For single node TRP monostatic sensing or single pair of bistatic sensing, both the radial velocity accuracy and the 3D velocity accuracy can be estimated. The true radial velocity is the projection of true 3D velocity on the direction from TRP to target. the sum of the following two components:

Component 1: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the unit-norm vector from target to STX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the STX;

Component 2: The projection of the velocity difference vector on the unit-norm vector from target to SRX, where the velocity difference vector is between the true 3D velocity of the target and the true 3D velocity of the SRX.

Note: If STx and SRx are static, true radial velocity is , where is the true 3D velocity of the target, is the bistatic angle between incident direction and scattering direction, and is the angle between the true 3D velocity vector and the bisector vector within the bistatic angle . For mono-static sensing, .

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.2-1a

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation, for object detection and/or tracking, regarding the performance target in UMa and Indoor Factory scenarios given by RAN Plenary:

The performance objective on False Alarm Probability (5%) applies to both False Alarm Probability Type 1 and False Alarm Probability Type 2.

The vertical localization accuracy requirement is only applicable to the case of sensing UAV.

Note that, multi-static can be constructed by one or more sensing modes with more than one STx and/or SRx. It is not precluded anyway.

In addition, Nokia proposes to remove 30 GHz for UMa and SMa, and Ofinno would like to consider 30 GHz as optional. FL thinks individual companies can choose the evaluated frequency but no need to prohibit an agreed combination. Panasonic suggests mapping different baseline combinations into cases in TR 22.837 and TR 22.870. FL thinks this does not change our evaluation scope with or without the mapping. Some other companies would like to modify the table, e.g. adding scenarios. Please refrain from such up-scoping at this stage due to the limited time.

Finally, the following proposal is made, based on the input. Some notes are also added to (1) clarify the intension, and (2) keep the door open for other sensing modes.

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.1-1

For Rel-20 6G ISAC performance evaluation of object detection and/or tracking, the following sensing modes are considered for the baseline cases/combinations.

[TABLE]
Sensing target | Indoor or outdoor | Scenario | Carrier frequency | Sensing mode
UAV | Outdoor | UMa-AV, SMa | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz (for UMa-AV only) | TRP mono-static, TRP-TRP bi-static
Human | Outdoor | UMa | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static
Human | Indoor | Indoor Hotspot | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static
Vehicle | Outdoor | Urban Grid, Highway | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz (for Urban Grid only) | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static
AGV | Indoor | Indoor factory (SH) | 4 GHz, ~7 GHz, 30 GHz | TRP-UE bi-static, UE-TRP bi-static

Note: Other sensing modes (including multi-static sensing) for a baseline case/combination are not precluded.

Qualcomm | Qualcomm | No | No | We don’t agree with the effort to explicitly limit to specific sensing modes the evaluation and we believe this table will be used out of context for downscoping specific sensing modes. We also observe that TRP-UE has been included in multiple outdoor and indoor scenarios. What will we learn additionally by having the same sensing mode in multiple scenarios? TRP-UE and UE-TRP have a lot of similarities; mainly the Tx power is different; the rest are the same. Instead of having both modes everywhere, at least for one of scenarios UE monostatic should be included. In the above list, it appears as if the TRP-UE/UE-TRP are the most important scenarios, however, from our understanding, these sensing mode suffers the most from bad LOS condition and will have limited performance in many cases. TRP-based sensing modes or UE-monostatic will have much better LOS conditions in several of these deployments.
